Class Archmage

java.lang.Object
core.Piece
pieces.Archmage
All Implemented Interfaces:
Moveable, Shootable
public class Archmage extends Piece implements Shootable
Represents an Archmage piece within the Kuroshiro engine. The Archmage is an upgraded magic caster with limited mobility (moving one square in any direction to empty spaces only) but possessing a devastating ranged attack. Its magic strikes in straight orthogonal lines over an infinite distance, but is balanced by a strict cooldown system.

  • Constructor Details

    • Archmage

      public Archmage(Color color, Position position)
      Initializes a new Archmage piece. The magic attack is fully charged and available immediately upon creation (the initial cooldown is set to 0).
      Parameters:
      color - the Color of the Archmage
      position - the initial starting Position on the board

  • Method Details

    • getLegalMoves

      public List<Position> getLegalMoves(Board board)
      Calculates all legal movement destinations for the Archmage. The Archmage moves similarly to a standard King (one square in any of the 8 directions), but strictly to unoccupied squares. It cannot capture enemies via physical movement.
      Specified by:
      getLegalMoves in interface Moveable
      Specified by:
      getLegalMoves in class Piece
      Parameters:
      board - the current state of the game Board
      Returns:
      a list of valid, empty Position coordinates the Archmage can step to

    • getShootTargets

      public List<Position> getShootTargets(Board board)
      Identifies all valid enemy targets the Archmage can currently strike with magic. If the ability is on cooldown, this immediately returns an empty list. Otherwise, it casts rays orthogonally outward until it hits the first piece in each direction. If that piece belongs to the opponent, it is added as a valid target.
      Parameters:
      board - the current state of the game Board
      Returns:
      a list of Position coordinates containing valid enemy targets

    • shoot

      public Piece shoot(Position target, Board board)
      Executes the infinite-range magic attack on a designated target position. Successfully firing the magic puts the ability on a 2-turn cooldown. If the target is a Warrior, it applies a damage state. For all other piece types, the target is immediately captured and removed from the board.
      Specified by:
      shoot in interface Shootable
      Parameters:
      target - the Position to shoot at
      board - the current state of the game Board
      Returns:
      the Piece that was captured or damaged, or null if the target was invalid or the magic is currently on cooldown

    • getTargets

      public List<Position> getTargets(Board board)
      Fulfills the Shootable interface requirement by providing the list of valid magic targets.
      Specified by:
      getTargets in interface Shootable
      Parameters:
      board - the current state of the game Board
      Returns:
      a list of valid target Position coordinates

    • canUseMagic

      public boolean canUseMagic()
      Evaluates if the Archmage's magic attack is fully recharged and ready to fire.
      Returns:
      true if the cooldown is at 0, false otherwise

    • getMagicCooldown

      public int getMagicCooldown()
      Retrieves the current number of turns remaining on the magic cooldown timer.
      Returns:
      the current cooldown integer value

    • decrementCooldown

      public void decrementCooldown()
      Decrements the magic cooldown timer by one turn, clamping at a minimum of 0. This method is typically invoked by the engine at the end of a turn cycle.

    • getSymbol

      public String getSymbol()
      Returns the unique symbol used to represent the Archmage in text-based rendering.
      Specified by:
      getSymbol in interface Moveable
      Specified by:
      getSymbol in class Piece
      Returns:
      the string "X"
